Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

During the promotional week, the customer will pay 15% less for each item. The handcrafted table originally priced at $200 will be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $170. The factory-made chairs originally priced at $150 will be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $127.50. The total amount the customer will pay for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. Reach for the discount, and the customer will enjoy the savings on both items. ***

Sarah's shop is a great place to support local artisans and workers, and the promotional week is a way to show appreciation for their hard work. The customer's purchase of the table and chairs is a perfect example of how supporting local businesses can lead to both financial benefits and a sense of community. The discount applied to each item ensures that the customer gets a fair deal while still supporting the factory workers. ***

The promotional week not only benefits the customer but also helps the factory workers earn more income. The discount on the table and chairs makes the purchase more affordable, and the customer can enjoy the savings without compromising on quality. The customer's purchase is a great example of how small actions can make a big difference in supporting local businesses.
